@Edent ah, interesting - was the Hub 5 something you had to request from Virgin, or did you just get one elsewhere and set it up? (As per recent discussion was issued with a 5x, with its lack Modem Mode.)

@yvan I already had the Hub 4. I went onto their online chat and whinged about poor download speeds until they sent me a new Hub.
If you're on the XPON network, I don't think you have a choice though.

@Edent ah yeah, just been trying to do some research, but it's all a bit muddy... I'm FTTP/XGS-PON so I think just stuck with the 5x... not an insurmountable problem, just a little annoying. (But compared to unreliable <40mpbs BT rotting fenland copper that cost me more than Virgin... I'm not really complaining.)

source_doc:
I'm doing a eekly 1hr meeting where I'm going over with a director of engineering my soap box on managing a product management organization and actually it's coming out to be a pretty good episodic thing

Topics have been
- different types of product managers and the basic skill sets in developing requirements
- how product managers ensure an ongoing cadence for an engineering team
- dealing with product managers with varying backgrounds (engineering, data analyst, operational SME, consulting etc)
what's been fun is that this Director and I worked at a previous company with product managers that I didn't manage and I'm able to reference "hey remember that person? that person was awesome and here's why."

so in various email chains and slack threads I'll forward them to said director (if he's already not in there) and be like "ok a GOOD product manager would do THIS" and a "BAD product maanger would do THAT" rememember when so and so did THIS? that was good.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Mun lean doallamin vahkkosaš 1 diimmu čoahkkima, gos geahčan ovttas engineering-direktoriin mu iežan “soap box” jurdagiid das mo jođihit product management-organisašuvnna, ja duođaid dát lea šaddamin hui buorre episodalaš juttu

Fáttát leat leamašan
- iešguđetlágan product managerat ja vuođđogálggat gáibádusaid ovddideamis
- mo product managerat sihkkarastet ahte engineering-joavkui lea joatkevaš rhythmma
- mo gieđahallat product managereid geain leat iešguđetlágan duogáštat (engineering, data-analysta, operatiiva SME, consulting jna)
mii lea leamašan suohtas, lea ahte dát direktor ja mun barggaimet ovddit fitnodagas product manageraiguin maid mun in jođihan, ja mun sáhtán čujuhit “hei, muistat go dan olbmo? dat olmmoš lei hui buorre ja dánin.”

de iešguđet e-mail-lánccain ja Slack-ságastallamiin mun ovddidan daid dán direktoriidda (jus son ii leat doppe jo) ja dadjan dego “ok BUORRE product manager dahká DÁNA” ja “HEAĐES product manager dahká DÁDA” muistat go goas nuppit ja nuppit dagai DÁNA? dat lei buorre.

source_doc:
@Maker_of_Things
Is there any bruising or swelling? If it’s swelling then let the practice know and keep an eye on it.
Assuming she took it from the veins around the elbow, theres a chance she went too deep and hit the back wall of the vein, and an underlying tendon then bruised it on the way out. All depends on technique and equipment. I could never get the hang of a fixed needle draw but was a deft hand at a butterfly draw, you could be much kinder.
Hope it’s eased now.

@tempusfelix
Yeah, from my elbow. It is still sore, but getting better. No bruising or bleeding though.

@Maker_of_Things
Good. She may well have been too deep so remember if they’re not urgent, you can often ask for a specific practitioner if your surgery allows it. For example I like the sister at mine, she’s so smooth.

Jos son váldii dan su cuozain gieđačoahkku birra, de sáhttá leat ahte son manai menddo vuollái ja deahkkai cuozza duohkenbeahttá, ja vuollisvuođas leatvuohta de sáhttii dagahit sinjuhusa go bođii olggos. Buot lea vuođđuduvvon teknikkas ja biergasiin. Mun in goassege oahppan bures geavahit giddes njuovččadanváldima, muhto lean lean buorre butterfly-váldimis, dat sáhttá leat olu lášmmohit.
Doivvon ahte dat lea dál buorránan.

Juo, mu gieđačoahkkus. Dat lea vel bávččas, muhto lea buorráneamen. Ii leat goitge sinjuhus dahje varraneapmi.

Buot lea dan alde makkár teknihkka ja rusttat leat geavahusas. Mun in goassege beassan bures oahppat dakkár giddes njuikimiin varra váldima, muhto butterfly-njuikimiin lean hui čeahpes, dat sáhttá leat olu geahppasut.
Doivvon ahte dat lea dál geahppánan.

@tempusfelix
Juo, mu gieđačoahkkus. Dat lea vel bávččas, muhto buorráneamen. Ii leat goitge siniheapmi dahje varran.

@Maker_of_Things
Bures. Son sáhttii bures leat mannan menddo vuollái, nu ahte muitte ahte jos dat ii leat hoahppu, de sáhtát dávjá jearrat dihto divššára, jos din doaktárdoibma dan diktá. Ovdamearkka dihte mun liikon min buohccedivššárii, son lea nu seavdnjat ja čeahpes.

source_doc:
Whoa, you mean Blender Market, the thing I said was the first step towards the Blender environment becoming a shitty subscription service, renamed itself to the oh-so-appealing name "Superhive" and then decided to mandate subscriptions?
WHOA WHO COULD HAVE GUESSED?! Not me, that's for sure. This is completely out of left field!

TBH, I have never seen a single company with "Hive" in the name that wasn't a complete shitshow run by assholes, so I never bought anything on that platform -
Sorry
I didn't RENT anything on that platform if I could help it.
Maybe it's unfair, but "clearly we, a company that specializes in connecting creators with their audience, should name ourselves HIVE, because the creators are just our loyal little bees, and audience is our loyal little bees, work for us!" always gives me bad vibes.

"Hive implies busy crowds!"
Hive implies a queen.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Voi, dat mearkkaša ahte Blender Market, dat mii mun dadjen lei vuosttaš lávki dan guvlui ahte Blender-birrasa šaddá vuollánis abonemeanta-bálvalussan, rievddadii nama dakkár ah sohkabealle vuostáiváldin “Superhive”-ii ja de mearridii geatnegastit abonemeanttaid?
VOI GII DIEDII LEAT SÁHTÁN ARVAT?! Ii mun, dat lea vissis. Dat bođii ollásit almmá ovddit várutusa!

Duhtavaččat dadjan, in leat goassege oaidnán ovtta ge fitnodaga mas lea “Hive” namas mii ii livčče ollásit heajos cirkus maid idihtat jođihit, nu ahte in goassege oastán mihkkege dan platformtas -
Ándagas
in LIIGAN mihkkege dan platformtas jos lean sáhttán dan garvit.
Soaitá leat eahpevuoigatlaš, muhto “čielgasit mii, fitnodat mii erenoamážit čatná ráhkadeddjiid iežaset geahččiide, galgat gohčodit iežamet HIVE:n, danne go ráhkadeddjit leat dušše min oskkáldas unna bealjit, ja geahččit leat min oskkáldas unna bealjit, barggidehket midjiide!” addá mulle álo heajos vibeaid.

“Hive mearkkaša ahte leat barggus olu olbmot!”

source_doc:
The fried eggs have turned to scrambled eggs.
You know what?
Considering we didn't have a spatula, it's not horrible.
Yum.
Alrighty.
It's breakfast time.
It is 904 and we're still at 100%.
To be fair, we didn't really do anything with the phone just yet.
It was just mostly sitting there in my pocket.
Also, yes, I do put the the milk in first because this way the cereal doesn't get soggy.
Unfortunately, it seems my opinion is a rather unpopular one.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Bássanmunat leat šaddan ruoskamasmunnan.
Dieđátgo maid?
Goit galgá dadjat ahte mis ii lean lasta, de ii leat nu hirbmat.
Njam.
Noh.
Lea iđitboradeami áigi.
Dat lea 9:04 ja mii leat ain 100 proseanttas.
Vuoiggalašvuođa dihte, mii eai duođas vel bargan mihkkege telefovnnaiguin.
Dat lei eanas dušše mu buksalihkus.
Ja, juo, mun bidjan mielkki vuos, danin go nu eai šatta cereal njammásin.
Heajos, orru mu oaivil lea viehka epaoabbás.
hypo_1:
Pannesteuvvon munat leat šaddan vuosttažettiin goikebas ruškojeaddji munan.
Dieđátgo maid?
Goit fertejit mii leat ilá go mis ii lean lastta, de ii leat dat nu hui bahá.
Njam.
Noh de.
Lea iđitboradeami áigi.
Kello lea 9:04, ja mii leat ain 100 proseanttas.
Vuoiggalašvuođa dihtii, mii eai leat vel duođaid bargan mihkkege telefovnnaiguin.
Dat lei eanas vuosttažettiin dušše mu lihkus.
Ja juo, mun bijan mielkki ovdal, danin go nu cereal ii šat njálggo.
Heajos, orru mu oaivil lea viehka epaoabbás.
hypo_2:
Bassejuvvon munat leat šaddan máhccanmunan.
Dieđát go maid?
Go mis ii lean steaikaspáhtta, de ii dat leat nu hui bahá.
Njam.
Noh.
Lea iđitboradanáigi.
Kello lea 9:04, ja mii leat ain 100 proseanttas.
Jus galgá vuoiggalaččat dadjat, de mii ean leat vel duođaid bargan telefovnnaiguin mihkkege.
Dat lei eanas áiggi dušše mu pohccas.
Ja juo, mun bijan mielkki vuos, danin go dán vuogi mielde cereal ii njuoska.
Heajos, orru ahte mu oaivil ii leat nu hui beakkán.
hypo_3:
Bassemunat leat šaddan vuodjanmunan.
Dieđát go maid?
Go mis ii lean lávot, de ii dat leat nu hirbmat fastá.
Njam.
Noh de.
Lea iđitboradanáigi.
Kello lea 9:04, ja mii leat ain 100 proseanttas.
Jus galgá vuoiggalaččat dadjat, de mii ean leat vel bargan telefovnnaiguin mihkkege.
Dat lei eanas áiggi dušše mu pohccas.
Ja juo, mun bijan mielkki vuos, danin go dán láhkai cereal ii šatta njuoskan.
Heajos orru ahte mu oaivil ii leat nu hui beakkán.
hypo_4:
Vuosttahuvvon munat leat šaddan munadivvun.
Dieđát go maid?
Go mis ii lean vuodjinskovli, de ii dat leat nu hirbmat.
Njam.
Noh.
Lea iđitboradanáigi.

source_doc:
Traction meets fashion: The best weatherproof trainers for rain-slicked pavements

The Cloudhorizon Waterproof feels purpose-built for the stop-start rhythm of winter commuting. Its lightweight waterproof upper keeps your feet dry without adding bulk, and the shoe's smooth rocker shape helps you roll forward easily, even when you're layered up and carrying heavy loads.

Underfoot, Helion foam creates a cushioned bounce that softens long walks between stations, while the grippy rubber outsole keeps things steady on wet steps and shiny tiles. The pared-back design gives it a polished, modern look, ideal if you prefer a trainer that handles lousy weather subtly, without shouting its technical credentials.

Cloudhorizon Waterproof orru leat sihke ráhkaduvvon dálvejohtolaga bissehii ja johttimii heivehan. Dan geahpes čáhcedievdduhan bajábealli doallá juolggit goikadin almmá lassáneaddji stuorusvuođa haga, ja vuodjinga smooth rocker-hápmi veahkeha du njoammilit ovddosguvlui álkit, vel go leat biepmuheapmin gárven ja guoddát losses noaidde.

Juolgevuolde Helion-fápmu ráhkada pehmeš biehttaleaddji dovddu, mii geahpeda guhkes vázzimiid stášuvnnaid gaskkas, muhto njuoskkas ruber vuolesuola doallá lávkkit nanusin njuoska ráidduin ja čuvges tile-guovlluin. source_doc:
Tinkering with my stereo system this week has got me thinking about an oddity that I've never been able to get to the bottom of...let's see if the Mastodon hive mine can help.
The amp is a Philips FA454 and the CD Player is a Philips CD150.
According to the amp manual I can connect the units with a "8-pole DIN to 5-pole DIN + 2 x single-pole CINCH" cable."
I take this to mean an 8 pin din to a 5 pin + stereo RCA cable...which sounds cursed.
Never been able to find one. Can anyone make one?

The service manual for the amp is nice and clear (picture 2 in above toot), but I can't find anything as nice for the CD player. I've attached a picture (picture 4 in the above toot) from the service manual that I *think* is the relevant section.
Currently I have the CD player hooked up with just the RCA cables and it all works fine, but if I could find/make the correct Din to Din + RCA cable then I could use some additional functionality and free up the only RCA stereo inputs on my amp.

There's a repair café this weekend in the town where I live...if I'm still puzzling over it all by then I might take it all along there and see if they can help me hack together a cable.

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Lean dovddastallan stereo-vuogádatain mánán vahkus, ja dat lea panen mu jurddašit earenoamáš odditvuođa birra maid in leat goassege beassan čielggadit...geahččalitgo jus Mastodon-jovku sáhttá veahkehit.
Amplifiere lea Philips FA454 ja CD-čuojan lea Philips CD150.
Ampa manuala mielde sáhtán ovttastit ovttadagaid “8-naba DIN to 5-naba DIN + 2 x okta-naba CINCH” kábelin."
Mun ipmirddan dan nu ahte lea 8-pin DIN 5-pin + stereo RCA kábelii...mii gullá veaháš garrahuvvon.
In leat goassege gávdnan dakkár. Sáhttágo oktage dahkat dakkár?

Ampa service-manual lea hui čielggas (govva 2 bajil mainna toot:s), muhto in gávnna seammá buori oktage CD-čuojánii. Lean mielddusin bidjan govva (govva 4 bajil mainna toot:s) service-manualas, mii *mu mielas* lea dat relevánta oassi.
Dál lean čatnan CD-čuojá just RCA-kábeliguin, ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jus sáhtášin gávdnat/dahkat rivttes DIN to DIN + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtin lassedoaimmaid ja oažžut friddjan amplifierea aino stereo RCA-sisaid.

Dán vahkoloahpa lea repair café gávpogis gos orron...jus lean vel dan birra oaivvildan dalle, de soaittán váldit buot doppe mielde ja geahččalit sáhttetgo sii veahkehit mu smiehttat ovtta kábela oktii.
hypo_1:
Lean dán vahku čohkkán stereo-vuogádagan lávlagiin, ja dat lea ožžon mu jurddašit ovtta hámis odditvuođa birra maid in leat goassege geargan čielggadit...geahččalupmi jus Mastodon-jovku sáhttá veahkehit.
Amplifiere lea Philips FA454 ja CD-čuojan lea Philips CD150.
Ampa manuala mielde sáhtán ovttastit ovttadagaid “8-naba DIN to 5-naba DIN + 2 x okta-naba CINCH” kábelin."
Mun ipmirddan dan nu ahte dat mearkkaša 8-pin DIN 5-pin + stereo RCA kábelii...mii gullá hui hirbmat.
In leat goassege gávdnan dakkár. Sáhttágo oktage dahkat ovtta?

Ampa service-manual lea hui buorre ja čielggas (govva 2 bajil olevas toot:s), muhto in gávnna seammá buori dieđu CD-čuojá várás. Lean mielddusin bidjan govva (govva 4 bajil olevas toot:s) service-manualas, mii *mu mielas* lea dat guoskevaš oassi.
Dál lea CD-čuojan čatnon dušše RCA-kábeliguin, ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jus sáhtášin gávdnat dahje dahkat rivttes DIN to DIN + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtun lassedoaimmaid ja seammás oažžut friddjan ampas daid áidna stereo RCA-sisaid.

Dán vahkoloahpa lea repair café dán gávpogis gos orron...jus lean vel ávvudan dan birra dalle, de soaittán váldit buot doppe mielde ja geahččalit sáhttetgo sii veahkehit mu ráhkadit ovtta kábela.
hypo_2:
Lean dán vahku duosttain stereo-vuogádagan, ja dat lea ožžon mu jurddašit ovtta odda ášši birra maid in leat goassege geargan čielggadit...geahččalupmi jus Mastodon-jovku sáhttá veahkehit.
Amplifiere lea Philips FA454 ja CD-čuojan lea Philips CD150.
Ampa manuala mielde sáhtán ovttastit dán ovttadagaid “8-naba DIN to 5-naba DIN + 2 x okta-naba CINCH” kábelin."
Mun ipmirddan dan nu ahte dat mearkkaša 8-pin DIN 5-pin + stereo RCA-kábela...ja dat gullá veahá garra surran.
In leat goassege gávdnan dakkár. Sáhttágo oktage dahkat ovtta?

Ampa service-manual lea buorre ja čielggas (govva 2 bajábeale toot:s), muhto in gávnna CD-čuojá várás seamma buori dieđu. Lean mielddusin bidjan govva (govva 4 bajábeale toot:s) service-manualas, mii *mu mielas* lea dat guoskevaš oassi.
Dál lea CD-čuojan čatnon dušše RCA-kábeliguin, ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jus sáhtášin gávdnat dahje ráhkadit rivttes DIN to DIN + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtun lassedoaimmaid ja seammás oažžut friddjan daid áidna stereo RCA-sisaid mat leat mu ampas.

Dán vahkoloahpa lea repair café dán gávpogis gos orron...jus lean vel áimmahuššame dan birra dalle, de soaittán váldit buot doppe mielde ja geahččalit sáhttetgo sii veahkehit mu ovtta kábela ráhkadit.
hypo_3:
Lean dán vahku ruhtadan iežan stereo-vuogádaga, ja dat lea ožžon mu jurddašit ovtta vuostálas ášši birra maid in leat goassege geargan čielggadit...geahččot vel jus Mastodon-jovku sáhttá veahkehit.
Amp lea Philips FA454 ja CD-čuojan lea Philips CD150.
Ampa manuala mielde sáhtán ovttastit ovttadagaid “8-naba DIN to 5-naba DIN + 2 x okta-naba CINCH” kábelin."
Mun ipmirddan dan nu ahte dat mearkkaša 8-pin DIN 5-pin + stereo RCA-kábela...mii gullá hui vearran.
In leat goassege gávdnan dakkár. Sáhttágo oktage ráhkadit ovtta?

Ampa service-manual lea buorre ja čielggas (govva 2 bajábeale toot:s), muhto in gávnna CD-čuojá várás seamma čielga dieđu. Lean mielddusin bidjan govva (govva 4 bajábeale toot:s) service-manualas, mii *mu mielas* lea dat guoskevaš oassi.
Dál lea CD-čuojan čatnon dušše RCA-kábeliguin, ja buot doaibmá bures, muhto jus sáhtášin gávdnat dahje ráhkadit rivttes DIN to DIN + RCA-kábela, de sáhtášin geavahit muhtun lassedoaimmaid ja seammás oažžut friddjan daid áidna stereo RCA-sisaid mat leat mu ampas.

instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. hypo_0:

Mun lean álo vuoidanbáhcán.
Maiddái, mun vajáldahtin mainit dan, muhto mun háliidan ahte dát vlogga lea iešráđđejeaddji olbmo beaivvi eallin.
Mun oaččuin dán ođđa unna pohkakámehra, ja mun lean nu ilus dan birra.
Nu.
Mun dušše válddán dásan muhtun sisdoalu.
Mana ollásit bajimužžii, boađe ravdii vuoddagiidatguin.
Perfekta.
Ja de bidjat mu monni duinna guovdil, ja dego guođe dan rahpatkeahttá.
Ja de, jo, stole veahá peittä zippera.

Perfekta.

Ja de, jo, stole veahá peittä zippera.

source_doc:
Progress after a while off. I got the sewing machine out to do the bill and a crown seam. Half the crown sewn plus the bill, resting atop the paper form made from the pattern. I had to rip and re-sew the machine stitch because I pinned along instead of across the seam and went off track. I might re-do the bill if I want a little more depth.
#millinery

Eight crown panels and two bill panels assembled into three pieces and pinned for general shape. Sachi's Studio Newsboy Cap pattern in green velvet. Mostly machine stitched.
The pattern calls for topstitching along the seams of the crown panels, which might suggest either matching or contrasting thread.
#millinery

Top view before I sew the crown halves together. Reviewing the how-to video. I see Sachi is using a few tiny spring clips during sewing instead of pinning the whole seam.

A bit of a messy join on the eight panels coming together at the peak of the newsboy cap, so I am thinking a larger button may be in order to disguise the seams.

Crown fully pieced, with cloth covered.button at peak. Raw edges loosely tucked under. Seams neither pressed nor topstitched.
#millinery
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Ovdananáigi maŋŋel unna botta eret. Válddán suodniuvvagievrra olggos dahkat skierpma ja kruvnna soadji. Bealli kruvnna lea suodjaluvvon ja skierbma maid, ja dat lea bábirhámis mii lea ráhkaduvvon mínstaris. Mun fertiin gaikut ja ođđasit suodjalit mašiinnasuodjama, danne go bihttájin biinnuid soadji mielde iige rastá soadji, ja manin geavahuvvui boastut. Sáhtán ođđasit dahkat skierpma jos háliidan veahá eanet čikŋodaga.
#gápmagahpadeapmi

Gávcci kruvnnapanela ja guokte skierbmapanela leat čoahkkáhuvvon golmma oasis ja bihtton oktii vai oažžu oppalaš hámi. Sachi's Studio Newsboy Cap mínstar ruoná seahkkavelvehtas. Eanemusat mašiinnain suodjaluvvon.
Mínstar bivdá bálddalassuodjama kruvnnapanelaid soadjigiid mielde, mii sáhttá mearkkašit sihke seamma ivnni dahje vuostálastteaddji láigá.
#gápmagahpadeapmi

Bajilčalbmi ovdal go suodjalan kruvnna bealuid oktii. Geahččan oahppovideo fas. Oainnán ahte Sachi geavaha muhtun unna giđđagieđahallansliivvaid suodjadettiin iige bihtte olles soadji.

Veahá seagas čoahkkaneapmi go gávcci panela boahtiba oktii newsboy-gáhpira báhccái, nu ahte jurddašan ahte stuorát boallu sáhttá leat buorre čiehkadit soadjigiid.

Kruvdna lea ollásit čoahkkáhuvvon, ja báhcás lea baksafáttágođiid geavaheaddji boallu. Rávdaeavttut leat geahpasit vuojuhuvvon vulos. Soadjit eai leat geahppon dahje bálddalassuodjaluvvon.
#gápmagahpadeapmi
hypo_1:
Ovdanupmi maŋŋel go lei veahá guhkki botta gaskkas. Válddán suodjanmašiinna olggos vai sáhtán dahkat skierpma ja ovtta kruvnna soadji. Bealli kruvnnas lea juo suodjaluvvon, seammás go skierbma, ja dat veallá bábirhámi alde mii lea ráhkaduvvon mínstaris. Mun fertiin gaikot suodjama ja suodjalit ođđasit mašiinnain, danne go bihtten soadji mielde iige rastá dan, ja nu manin soadji manai veahá boastut. Sáhttán maid dahkat skierpma ođđasit, jos háliidan dasa veahá eanet čikŋodaga.
#gáhpirduodji

Gávcci kruvnnapanela ja guokte skierbmapanela leat čoahkkáhuvvon golmma oasis, ja bihtton vai oppalaš hápmi oidnu. Sachi's Studio Newsboy Cap mínstar ruoná velvehtas. Eanemusat mašiinnain suodjaluvvon.
Mínstar celko ahte galgá bálddalassuodjalit kruvnnapanelaid soadjigiid mielde, mii sáhttá mearkkašit sihke seamma ivnni láigá dahje čielga vuostálas ivnni láigá.
#gáhpirduodji

Bajáldasgeahččal ovdal go suodjalan kruvnna bealuid oktii. Geahčadan oahppovideo fas. Oainnán ahte Sachi geavaha muhtun unna giđđesliivvaid suodjadettiin iige bihtte olles soadji.

Veahá seagasin lea dat čoahkkaneapmi, gos gávcci panela boahtiba oktii newsboy-gáhpira báhccái, nu ahte jurddašan ahte stuorát boallu sáhttá leat buorre čiehkadit soadjigiid.

Kruvdna lea ollásit čoahkkáhuvvon, ja báhcás lea lávkkain gokčojuvvon boallu. Rávdaeavttut leat geahpasit vuojuhuvvon vuolde. Soadjit eai leat geahppon iige bálddalassuodjaluvvon.
#gáhpirduodji

Ovdáneapmi maŋŋel go lean veahá boddui eret. Válddán gođđinmašiinna olggos vai dahkat skierpma ja ovtta kruvnnasoaji. Bealli kruvnna lea gođđojuvvon, ja skierbma maid, ja dat veallá bajábealde bábirhámi mii lea ráhkaduvvon mínstara mielde. Mun fertiin čoaskut mašiinnagođđoma ja gođđit dan ođđasit, danne go bihtten soaji mielde iige rastá dan, ja de manin boastut. Sáhttán skierpma ođđasit dahkat, jos háliidan dasa veahá eanet čikŋodaga.
#gáhpirduodji

Gávcci kruvnnapanela ja guokte skierbmapanela leat čoahkkáhuvvon golmma oasis ja bihtton vai ožžot oppalaš hámi. Sachi's Studio Newsboy Cap mínstar ruoná velvehtas. Eanemusat mašiinnain gođđojuvvon.
Mínstar bivdá bajildasgođđoma kruvnnapanelaid soadjigiid mielde, mii sáhttá mearkkašit ahte láigi lea seamma ivnnas dahje vuostálas ivnnas.
#gáhpirduodji

Bajáldasgeahččal ovdal go gođđán kruvnna bealuid oktii. Geahčadan oahppovideo fas. Oainnán ahte Sachi geavaha muhtun unna giđđesliibmáid gođđodettiin iige bihtte olles soaji.

Veahá seagasin lea oktanupmi go gávcci panela boahtiba oktii newsboy-gáhpira báhcás, nu ahte jurddašan ahte stuorát boallu livččii buorre čiehkadit soadjigiid.

Kruvdna lea ollásit čoahkkáhuvvon, ja báhcás lea boallu mii lea gođiin gokčojuvvon. Rávdaeavttut leat geahpasit vuojuhuvvon vuolde. Soadjit eai leat geahppon iige bajildasgođđojuvvon.
#gáhpirduodji

A couple of weeks ago, my boyfriend and I did pottery.
I mean, actual pottery.
And it's now time to collect our masterpieces.
So I'm in the office today, and it is literally just a 20 minute walk away from the office.
But the one time I went, I obviously used my phone map because I'd never been in that area before.
So let's hope I can remember some orientation skills and use this thing.

source_doc:
In a surprise Inspector Clouseau twist, I now have an extra thing to investigate for Spooky Kitties Sciences!
I was doing a quick scroll through the evenings secondary camera video, when I spotted a curious The Inspector standing on the trunk of the tree near the feeding station. Kept jumping around, finding him near where the magpies had been, so I initially suspect(edit) he was simply chasing them as they sometimes let him do, until I randomly chance stop...
#SpookyKittiesSciences

There Inspector Clouseau was, rolling around like crazing about 2 meters NE of the tree, mostly hidden by the unfortunate placement of the awnings east post. Still, there he was for 5 minutes, at which point he ran back up the tree, walked across the lawn to the hill, flattened himself at the bottom, and then ran off camera, presumably in the middle of The Zoomies.
2/
#SpookyKittiesSciences

Now, as some of my Spooky Kitties Sciences readers may know, the motivations behind that tests are not just to entertain the kitties. While the most important aspect is the genuine curiosity I had seeing the kitties rolling around in the snow, I really wanted to find something that The Inspector liked. I came out of the initial testing with only a single moment to hint that he liked something. What that was is still a mystery.
3/
#SpookyKittiesSciences

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Inspector Clouseau-faamolaš várakeahtes jorgaleamis lea mus dál vel okta lassidut ášši maid dutkat Spooky Kitties Sciences várás!
Mun lean iežan eahkeda sekundera kámeavideo jođánit skrollame, go oidnen áibbas vuogas The Inspector čuožžilit muorrastobdos borranbáikki lahka. Son lei joatkkalaččat báhccan birra, gávdnan su doppe gos skávllut ledje leamaš, ja danin álggus jáhkken(redigeren), ahte son dušše lea daid viehkanan, nu go dat muhtumin sugejit sutnje dahkat, dassážii go muhtun veara sahttehis bissehan...
#SpookyMizitDieđat

Doppe lei Inspector Clouseau, čirrolit iežas birra dego veahttásupmi sullii 2 mehtera muorras gitta norda-lulli bealde, eanas oassi čihkkojuvvon dan bahálaš lávvepostta nuppástuvvan sajádagas nuorttabealde. Liikká, son lei doppe 5 minuhta, ja de son viehkai ruovttoluotta bajás muorrái, váccii suoidnemiđu rastá várrái, deaddei iežas vuollái vuostá vuolde, ja de viehkai eret kámearas, ilmehahtti The Zoomies gaskkas.
2/

#PWHL #hizzackySeason BOS at MIN

#PWHL #hizzackySeason
Whatever Heise did to put a Fleet player on her butt at center ice, that Fleet player paid back HARD 10 seconds later. Yeesh. 😬🫣
On the one hand, I want the Fleet's goalie's PWHL debut to go well, but on the other hand, I want the Frost to win.
Kendall Coyne Schofield was PISSED at the front of the net with that little stick scramble after the Fleet's second goal. I'm glad to have her fury back on the ice. She is ferocious!

#PWHL #hizzackySeason

#PWHL #hizzackySeason
YES! THEY TIED THAT SHIT UP!
Nooooooo, the Fleet is back up.
Roller-coaster of a game, here!

#PWHL #hizzackySeason
Wowza, Fleet is *still* gunning for Heise. Yee-ouch. That's two Fleet penalties to that one center-ice unpenalized shove by Heise.

#PWHL #hizzackySeason
It is so so good to see Kendall Coyne Schofield parked back at the top of the crease, getting shoved around by women a head taller than her, yet pushing right back, making plays.

#PWHL #hizzackySeason
Crowd is not convinced from what we saw that Curl deserved a double minot for roughing.
Crowd is not happy about this illegal hit call against Fleet on 71's injury.

Boston's defense is just too good. They aren't giving us any openings.

source_doc:
Local stable out to cash in at Coffs with four contenders

Local trainer Noel Mayfield-Smith is hoping to have a lucrative day at Sunday's Coffs Harbour meeting with four runners stepping out for the stable on the six-race card.

The Group 1-winning horseman will start his day in the second event of the day when Ausbred Yahoo contests the Komatsu Country Boosted Benchmark 58 Handicap (1605m) with two-kilogram claiming apprentice Ella Drew taking the ride.

Racing returns to Coffs Harbour on Sunday.

Ausbred Yahoo has been racing in good form with a third placing at Kempsey three starts ago being followed by two successive seconds at Coffs Harbour, the latest when beaten by Sunday's rival Caledonian.

Mayfield-Smith links with another apprentice, three-kilogram claimer Leanne Boyd, when Never Island starts in the Insurance Advisernet Australia Pink Silks F&M Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m).

A winner of three of her 15 starts, Never Island will carry 59.5 kilograms after Boyd's allowance. Never Island was beaten less than a length when second behind Montgomery at Grafton and backed that up with a last-start fifth at Coffs Harbour behind In The Fine Print when again beaten less than a length.

Raymond Spokes takes the ride on the Mayfield-Smith-prepared Monte Cruise in the O'Brien Plumbing Relining Wayne Glenn Pink Silks Cup Benchmark 82 Handicap (1205m).

Monte Cruise is also a three-time winner from just the nine starts. She hasn't raced since July when finishing last of eight at Eagle Farm and has had two trials at this track in preparation for her return, finishing second behind Saturday's Wyong winner Petticoat before winning the latest of them.

Mayfield-Smith rounds out the day when he again links with Boyd when she rides Cardsharp in the Key Community Group C,G&E Benchmark 58 Handicap (1305m).

hypo_0:
Báikkálaš ruvttostállu áigu Coffsas geavahit vejolašvuođa ruhtadit njeallje gilvválaččain

Báikkálaš hárjeheaddji Noel Mayfield-Smith doaivá oažžut buori dietnasa sotnabeaivvi Coffs Harboura gilvvuin, gos stállus leat njeallje heasta guđain gilvokártii, mas leat guhtta gilvvu.

Joavku 1-vuittiiguin beastán heastaolmmái álggaha beaivvi nuppi gilvvus, go Ausbred Yahoo gilvu Komatsu Country Boosted Benchmark 58 Handicap:s (1605 m), ja guokte-kilováttis geahččaleaddji Ella Drew vuodjá dan.

Gilvu máhccá Coffs Harbourii sotnabeaivvi.

Ausbred Yahoo lea gilván buori hámiin; golmma álggu maŋŋel dat lei goalmmát Kempsey:s, ja dan maŋŋel lea guokte háve čuovvovaččat leamaš nubbin Coffs Harbour:s, maŋemus háve go son vuollánai sotnabeaivvi vuosttálaš Caledonianii.

Mayfield-Smith bargá ovttas vel okta geahččaleaddjiin, golbma-kilováttis Leanne Boydain, go Never Island álggaha Insurance Advisernet Australia Pink Silks F&M Benchmark 58 Handicap:s (1305 m).

Never Island, mii lea vuoitán golbma geardde 15 álggus, guodda 59,5 kilo Boyd geassema maŋŋel. Never Island vuollánai unnit go ovtta guhkkodaga go lei nubbin Montgomery maŋis Grafton:s, ja dan maŋŋel son lei viđat maŋemuš álggus Coffs Harbour:s In The Fine Print maŋis, fas vuollánan unnit go ovtta guhkkodaga.

Raymond Spokes vuodjá Mayfield-Smitha ráhkadan Monte Cruise O'Brien Plumbing Relining Wayne Glenn Pink Silks Cup Benchmark 82 Handicap:s (1205 m).

Monte Cruise lea maid golbma geardde vuoiti dain dušše ovcci álgguin. Son ii leat gilván suoidnemánu rájes, go lei maŋemuš gávcci heastta joavkkus Eagle Farm:s, ja son lea leamaš guokte geahččaladdamis dán gilvobanas máhccanráhkadeapmin, gos son lei nubbin laurdde Wyong-vuitti Petticoat maŋis ovdal go vuittii maŋemus geahččaladdama.

Mayfield-Smith loahpaha beaivvi go son fas bargá ovttas Boydiin, go son vuodjá Cardsharp Key Community Group C,G&E Benchmark 58 Handicap:s (1305 m).

Son ii leat gilván suoidnemánu rájes, go son lei maŋimuš gávcci heastta gaskkas Eagle Farm:s, ja máhcadeami ovdal son lea leamaš guovtti geahččalangilvosis dán gilvobanas. Son lei nubbin laurdde Wyong-vuoiti Petticoat maŋis ovdal go vuittii maŋimuš geahččalangilvvuin.

Mayfield-Smith loahpaha beaivvi go son fas bargá ovttas Boydain, guhte vuodjá Cardsharp Key Community Group C,G&E Benchmark 58 Handicap-gilvui (1305 m).
hypo_6:
Báikkálaš stállu háliida fuolahit Coffsas njeallji gilvaleaddjiin

Báikkálaš hárjeheaddji Noel Mayfield-Smith doaivá ahte son oažžu buori dienáhusbeaivvi sotnabeaivvi Coffs Harboura gilvobeaivvis, gos njeallje heastta su stállus oassálastet guhtta gilvvu prográmmas.

Joavku 1-vuittihan heastaolbmo beaivi álgá beaivvi nuppi gilvvus, go Ausbred Yahoo oassálastá Komatsu Country Boosted Benchmark 58 Handicap-gilvui (1605 m), ja guokte kilo geasseoasi ožžon oahppovuodji Ella Drew vuodjá.

Heasttagilvut máhccet Coffs Harbourii sotnabeaivvi.

Ausbred Yahoo lea gilván buori hámiin. Golbma álggu dás ovdal son lei goalmmát Kempsey:s, ja dan maŋŋel lea son leamaš guokte maŋŋálagaš nubbin Coffs Harbour:s, maŋimuš geardde go vuollánai sotnabeaivvi vuosttálažžanii Caledoniani.

Mayfield-Smith bargá maiddái ovttas eará oahppovuodjiin, golbma kilo geasseoasi ožžon Leanne Boydain, go Never Island álggaha Insurance Advisernet Australia Pink Silks F&M Benchmark 58 Handicap-gilvui (1305 m).

Never Island lea vuoitán golbma iežas 15 álggus, ja Boyd geasseoasi maŋŋel son guodda 59,5 kilo. Grafton:s Never Island lei nubbin Montgomery maŋis ja vuollánai unnit go ovtta heastaguhkkodaga. Dan maŋŋel son lei viđát maŋimuš álggus Coffs Harbour:s In The Fine Print maŋis, fas vuollánan unnit go ovtta heastaguhkkodaga.

It'll be just like giving us allowance.

We were.

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Dát sáhttá leat veaháš eambbo go min dábálaš ruhta lei duođaid vel oktiibuot.

Mii bajásšaddat 90-logus go buot lei hálbi.

Juo.
Mana Discordii G g Wolf Den, ja don sáhtát, jos čatnat du Twitch-konttu, de doppe lea dušše doarjjuid várás kanála, ja mun lean bidjan liŋkkaid buot bonus-oasiide doppe.

source_doc:
and it's not a bad film — it's a spectacular-looking one in spots — but it is also a film entirely made out of other, better and more thematically cohesive films. so it's 2001 and the three-body problem and arrival and wall-e a little bit, but it's also Quips Sexyman Saves The World Part XVII and ultimately just not that lot

while watching i wondered why the earth needed to be in peril, and if the other two members of the multicultural crew dying to leave the White Man in charge was some kind of an anti-woke statement; but in light of some of the book author andy weir's 'most media literate libertarian' comments about how star trek should be just about spaceships going pew-pew, at least to him the answer is yes to the latter and 'fascism' to the former

@esvrld I read the book a little while back and I may have given him too much credit, especially given his recent decision to open his mouth, but I took the death of the international crew to strand the white boy as an understanding that his second book failed because he is not any good at writing people interacting with each other and his only skill is creating clever little puzzles for his self-insert to solve in complete isolation. Giving him an alien buddy was a good move because it opens the possibility of character interaction without requiring it to resemble the way humans relate to one another
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
ja ii dat leat heajos filmma — muhtun sajis lea hui spektakuláralaš geahččut — muhto dat lea maiddái filmma mii lea ollásit ráhkaduvvon eará, buoret ja temáhtalaččat oktiiheivvolaččat filmmaid birra. nu ahte dat lea 2001 ja golmma goruda problemma ja Arrival ja Wall-E veaháš, muhto dat lea maiddái Nállolassii Sexpmáinnas Geassema Máilmmi Oassi XVII ja loahpas ii leat nu ollu mihkkege

go mun geahččalin, jurddašin manne eatnama dárbbašii leat vaaras, ja ahte go dan máŋggakultuvrralaš joavkku guokte eará lahtu jápmiba dušše dan várás ahte Vilges Olmmái báhcá jođiheaddjin, leatgo dat muhtin anti-woke cealkámuš; muhto go váldá vuhtii girječálli Andy Weir:a “mediahálddaheapmásaččat lohkamearrásit libertára” kommentáraid das ahte Star Trek galggašii leat dušše űvssiid birra mat mannet pew-pew, de unnimusat sutnje vástádus lea jo das maŋimusas ja “fášisma” dan ovdalmainn

@esvrld logen girjji muhtin áiggi dás ovdal, ja soaitán lean addán sutnje liika ollu kredit, erenoamážit go vuhtii váldá su ođđa mearrádusa rahpat njálmmi, muhto mun dulkkojin riikkaidgaskasaš joavkku jápmima nu ahte vilges bárdni báhcá okto dan dihte ahte son ipmirdii iežas nubbi girji filtii danne go ii son leat mihkkege buorre čállit olbmuid gaskasaš ovttasdoaimmaid, ja su áidna gelbbolašvuohta lea ráhkadit jierpmis uhca pulcemaid maid su ieš-bidjan háhka galgá čoavdit ollásit earret earáin. Alien-ustiba addit sutnje lei buorre geažil, danne go dat rahpá vejolašvuođa persovnnalaš ovttasdoibmii, almmá dat ii dárbbaš leat sullasaš go olbmot gulahallet guhtet guimmiideaset
hypo_1:
ja ii dat leat heajos filmma — muhtin báikkiin dat lea hui čáppat geahččut — muhto dat lea maiddái filmma mii lea ollásit čoahkkáiduhttojuvvon eará filmmain, mat leat buoret ja temáhtalaččat ovttasdoallileappot. nu ahte dat lea veaháš 2001 ja The Three-Body Problem ja Arrival ja Wall-E, muhto dat lea maiddái Nállu Sexyman Geassema Máilmmi Oassi XVII, ja loahpas ii leat nu hirbmat ollu

go mun geahččalin, de jurddašin manne eatnan dárbbašii leat vaaras, ja leatgo dat ahte dan máŋggakultuvrralaš joavkku guokte eará lahtu jápmiba vai Vilges Olmmái báhcá jođiheaddjin, muhtin anti-woke cealkámuš; muhto go váldá vuhtii dan maid girječálli Andy Weir lea hupman iežas “mediačiekŋalis libertáralaš” kommentáriin das ahte Star Trek galggašii leat dušše űvssiid birra mat mannet pew-pew, de unnimusat sutnje vástádus lea maŋimusii jo, ja ovdalmaŋimuššii “fášisma”

@esvrld logen dan girjji muhtin áigge dás ovdal, ja soaitán lean addán sutnje liikaa ollu árvvu, erenoamážit go son ođđasit mearridii rahpat njálmmi, muhto mun dulkkojin dan riikkaidgaskasaš joavkku jápmima nu ahte vilges bárdni báhcá okto dan nugo ipmárdussan ahte su nubbi girji filtii danne go son ii leat mihkkege buorre čállit das go olbmot doibmet guhtet guimmiideaset, ja su áidna gelbbolašvuohta lea ráhkadit jierbmás uhca pulcemaid maid su iešbidjan-háhka galgá čoavdit ollásit okto. Alien-ustiba addit sutnje lei buorre fievrrádus, danne go dat rahpá vejolašvuođa persovdnalaš gulahallamii almmá dan haga ahte dat galggašii sulastahttit dan mii láhkái olbmot leat oktavuođas guhtet guimmiideaset
hypo_2:
ja ii dat leat heajos filmma — muhtin báikkiin dat lea duođaid spektakuláralaš geahččut — muhto dat lea maiddái filmma mii lea ollásit ráhkaduvvon eará filmmain, mat leat buoret ja temáhtalaččat čavččaseappot ovttas. nu ahte das lea veaháš 2001, The Three-Body Problem, Arrival ja Wall-E, muhto dat lea maiddái Nállunjálmmis sexyman geassa máilmmi, Oassi XVII, ja loahpas ii leatge nu hui ollu

go mun dan geahččalin, de jurddašin manne eatnan galggai leat vaaras, ja ahte go dan máŋggakultuvrralaš joavkku guokte eará lahtu jápmejit vai Vilges Olmmái báhcá jođiheaddjin, leago dat muhtin anti-woke cealkámuš; muhto go jurddaša girječálli Andy Weir:a “mediačiekŋaleamos libertárii” kommentáraid das ahte Star Trek galggašii leat dušše űvssiid birra mat mannet pew-pew, de unnimusat sutnje vástádus lea maŋimusii jo, ja ovdalmaŋimuššii “fašisma”

source_doc:
project move out, spring break relay
day 1:
Cleared main bedroom closet
& contents of boxes on one wall.
I'm surveying all my belongings. Pulling out a small percentage to save. Sentimental items. Practical pieces for putting together a new life. At this stage I know I'm selecting more than I need, probably more than I can carry; but the amount that I'm leaving behind is far greater. Deciding what to keep & where comes after the survey. All the rest will be turned over to "scavengers."
#moverR

Day 2, midday break, Monday.
Today is harder than yesterday. I'm uncovering far more "sentimental" stuff. The challenge: these saved memories are a physical drag that impair rather than contribute anything practically to my future AND I feel integrated looking at them. There's a strong general condemnation, I feel, of holding onto sentimental items. Yet I know that they are powerful. But hey, I'm an artist. Even wistful, sad memories are valuable to me. I'm proceeding as before, retaining less+

+than I'm tossing, while knowing that I am keeping "too much." The next phase will be the actual moving & storing phase when more might be cut. This phase is the pre-screen before turning the cleared items over to others who will take everything away.
File under: how to move out of a home of 12 years & a home town of 45 years, with family roots here (& artifacts) from 150 years. I'm finally leaving, in search of a culture that suits my values.
#moverR
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
fárrenprošeakta, giđđalohpi relay
beaivi 1:
Ráidon váldolanjá birgeskáhpa
& boksaid sisdoalu ovtta seaidnis.
Mun geahččan buot mu opmodagaid. Váidán eret unna proseantta maid vurken. Dovddalaš diŋggat. Geavatlaš diŋggat ođđa eallima huksemii. Dán muttos dieđán ahte válljen eanet go dárbbašan, ja várra eanet go sáhtán guoddit; muhto dat mearri maid guođán maŋis lea ollu stuoribut. Mii galgá vurkejuvvot & gokko, dan mearridan maŋŋel go lean geahčadan buot. Buot eará addojuvvo "čoaggideaddjiide."
#fárrenprošeaktaR

Beaivi 2, beaivemeari boddu, vuossárga.
Odne lea váddásut go eilen. Lean gávdnan olu eanet "dovddalaš" diŋggaid. Hástalus: dát vurkejuvvon muitalusat leat fysalaš noađu mii eai veahkit mu boahttevaš eallimis geavatlaččat, muhto eambbo hehttejit, JA mun dovddan ahte dat leat oassi mus go geahččan daid. Lean dovdán ahte dovddalaš diŋggaid vurkemis lea nanus oppalaš duššeheapmi. Dattege dieđán ahte dat leat fámolaččat. Muhto hei, mun lean dáiddár. Vel hávskes, morašlaš muitalusatge leat árvvus munnje. Lean joatkimin seamma láhkai, vurkkiin unnit+

+go maid bálkestan eret, vaikko dieđán ahte vurkken "liika olu." Boahtte muttos lea duohta fárren- & vurkenmuttos, go doppe sáhttá vel eanet čuohppot eret. Dát muttos lea ovdageahčadeapmi ovdal go adden ráidduhuvvon diŋggaid earáide geat váldet buot eret.
Bija dán vuollái: mo fárret eret ruovttus gos lean orron 12 jagi, ja ruovttugilis gos lean leamaš 45 jagi, mas leat bearraša ruohttasat dáppe (& artefávttat) 150 jagi rájes. Loahpalaččat lean fárremin eret, ohcame kultuvrra mii heive mu árvvuide.
#fárrenprošeaktaR

fárrenprošeakta, giđđalohpe-relay
beaivi 1:
Ráidejuvvon váldolanjá birgeskáhpa
& boksaid sisdoallu ovtta seaidnis.
Mun lean geahčadeamen buot mu opmodagaid. Válljen eret uhca proseantta maid vurket. Dovddalaš diŋggat. Geavatlaš diŋggat ođđa eallima huksemii. Dán muddui dieđán ahte válljen eanet go dárbbašan, várra eanet go sáhtán guoddit; muhto dat mearri maid guođán maŋi beallái lea ollu stuorát. Mearrideapmi dan birra mii galgá vurkejuvvot & gokko, boahtá maŋŋel go lean geahčadan buot. Buot eará addojuvvo "čoaggejeaddjiide."
#fárrenR

Beaivi 2, beaivemeari boddu, vuossárga.
Odne lea váddásut go eilen. Lean gávdname olu eanet "dovddalaš" diŋggaid. Hástalus: dát vurkejuvvon muitalusat leat fysalaš noađu mii eambbo hehtte mu boahttevaš eallima geavatlaččat go veahkeha, JA mun dovddan iežan ollislažžan go geahččan daid. Mu mielas lea gievra oppalaš suollemas dovddalaš diŋggaid vurkemis. Dattege dieđán ahte dat leat fámolaččat. Muhto hei, mun lean dáiddár. Vel hávski ja morašlaš muitalusatge leat munnje árvvus. Lean joatkimin seamma láhkai, vurkkiin unnit+

+go bálkestan eret, vaikko dieđán ahte vurkken "liika olu." Boahtte muttos lea duohta fárren- & vurkenmuttos, gos sáhttá vel eanet čuohppot eret. Dát muttos lea ovdageahčadeapmi ovdal go adden ráidduhuvvon diŋggaid earáide geat váldet buot eret.
Bija dán fiilla vuollái: mo fárret eret ruovttus gos lean leamaš 12 jagi & ruovttugilis gos lean leamaš 45 jagi, mas leat bearraša ruohttasat dáppe (& artefávttat) 150 jagi rájes. Loahpalaččat lean fárremin eret, ohcamin kultuvrra mii heive mu árvvuide.
#fárrenR

source_doc:
I'm so glad you're here.
Nothing better than sharing Christmas with friends and family.
I thought we could do the decorating together.
Is this the Earl of Shannon?
Indeed.
He's in the study.
Shall we?
Darling?
It's Isabella Caldwell and her lovely granddaughter.
Oh, won't she make someone a wonderful wife?

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. hypo_0:
Mun lean nu ilus ahte don leat dáppe.
Ii leat mihkkege buoret go juogadit juovllaid ustibiiguin ja bearrašiin.
Mun jurddašin ahte mii sáhtášii čohkket dekorerena ovttas.
Leago dát Earl of Shannon?
Juo.
Son lea bargohivssus.
Mannatgo?
Árran?
Dat lea Isabella Caldwell ja su hearvásáš áttán.
Ii go son šatta geasa nu buorre isitnán?
hypo_1:
Mun lean hui ilus ahte don leat dáppe.
Ii leat mihkkege buoret go juogadit juovllaid ustibiiguin ja bearrašiin.
Mun jurddašin ahte mii sáhtášii čohkket dán ovttas.
Leago dát Shannona earl?
Juo, dat lea nu.
Son lea bargolatnjás.
Vuolgitgo?
Ráhkis?
Dát lea Isabella Caldwell ja su čáppa áddjáčalbmi.
Ii go son šatta geasa nu buorre isitnán?
hypo_2:
Mun lean hui ilus ahte don leat dáppe.

Mun jurddašin ahte mii sáhtášimme čábbudit dán ovttas.

Juo, dat lea nu.
Son lea bargobáikkis.
Manaimet go?
Ráhkis?
Dát lea Isabella Caldwell ja su čáppa áhkkuvuođa nieida.
Oi, son gal šaddá geasa nu buorre isitnán.

source_doc:
@hacks4pancakes What do you think is actually going on?
Confused? Anxious? Executive Dysfunction? Unrealistic? Bone idle?
(I'm NOT confusing ND with idleness in my internal ontology here, but I wonder if either might be contributing...)

@hacks4pancakes @DamonHD Foe emigration at least, I can imagine myself saying “oh I’m so jealous, I would LOVE to do that” when the truth is there are internal or external reasons why I wouldn’t/couldn’t and someone offering to help me with the logistics wouldn’t change that so I politely decline.

@darryl @hacks4pancakes The grown-up way to politely decline would be "I got ahead of myself: I cannot do that for other reasons much as I'd like to", not brushing it off as a diary issue!

Established in 1860.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Dán hui buorre ja iešguđet láhkai stuorra seaŋggas lea hui buorre molsašupmi min unna telttii.
Geahča dán, olbmot.
Das leat seaŋgagáldut ja buot.
Juo, dát hotella lea hui boaris hotella, ja das lea olu iešvuohta.
Das leat nu olu boares stiillas báikemuittut.
Geahča dán čállinbeavddi.
Olmmoš gal hálida dušše suhkat doppe ja váldit čáhppesulgii alccesis ja álgit.
In dieđe, čállit reivvet dahje muhtin dakkár.
Vuođđuduvvon jagi 1860.
hypo_1:
Dán hirbmat stuorra seaŋggas, gos lei hui buorre nohkat, lea dat hui buorre molsašupmi min unna telttii.
Geahča dán, olbmot.
Dás leat seaŋgagáldut ja buot.
Juo, dát hotella lea hui boaris, ja das lea ollu iešvuohta.
Dás leat máŋga boares stiilla báikemuittu.
Geahča dán čállinbeavddi.
Olmmoš gal háliida dušše čohkkát doppe, váldit čáhppesulgii ovdan ja álgit.
In dieđe, čállit reivveid dahje vaikko maid.
Vuođđuduvvon 1860:s.

Dás leat seaŋggačuolppat ja buot.
Juo, dát hotella lea hui boares hotella, ja das lea hirbmat olu luondu.
Dás leat diekkár máŋga vintage-bárdnosa.
Geahča dán čállinbeavddi.
Dus gal boahtá hállu čohkkát doppe, váldit čállinsuli ovdan ja dušše álggahit.
In dieđe, čállit reivveid dahje vaikko maid.
Vuođđuduvvon jagi 1860.

Juo, dát hotella lea hui boares, ja das lea hui olu iešvuohta ja vuoigŋa.
Dás leat ollu boares stiillas báhccin bártniid ja áidnaid.
Geahča dán čállinbeavddi.
Dus šaddá hállu dušše čohkkát doppe, váldit čállinsuli ovdan ja álggahit.
In dieđe, čállit reivveid dahje vaikko maid.
Vuođđuduvvon jagi 1860.

source_doc:
@kagan Just use anything, even the cashier receipts from your grocery shopping - whatever you want. A friend of mine who exhibits a lot put a sheet of toilet roll from every place he's exhibiting in his scrapbook another friend got injured while hanging her frames and eventually used the cast from the injury and some gouache to stamp patters on the pages. Hence: everything's involved you want involved.

@aurora And then you... take all these things, and glue, staple, or otherwise attach them into a book? Is that it?
I'm unclear on what happens next, or why.
Honestly, I may just not be a scrapbooking person.

@kagan Yes, that's exactly the way to go! Intentionally spill your coffee, drop an ink cartridge... whatever suits you. One just needs to get all these fancy ideas shown on pinterest or whatever out of one's head. It's about documenting the process instead of being a visual dainty. Use it like a wrecking journal if that suits. It's totally up to the person who makes it.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
@kagan Geavat maid beare, vel du biebmoostanreissusas kassabáhpáriid - maid beare háliidat. Mu ustibat, guhte olu čájáha, bidjá ieš scrapbookii báhpárbihttá wc-báhpáris juohke sajis gos son lea čájeheamen, muhtun eará ustibat bávččii go lei heiveheamen rámmaidis ja loahpas geavahii gipsa bávččas ja veahá gouachea deaddilit minstariid siidduide. Danin: buot sáhttá leat mielde maid háliidat mielde.

@aurora Ja de don... válddát buot dán, ja liibmat, stávvalat, dahje eará láhkai čatnat daid girjái? Lea dat nu?
Mus ii leat čielga mii de dáhpáhuvvá dan maŋŋá, dahje manin.
Duosttažettiin, sáhttá leat ahte mun in leat scrapbook-olmmoš ollesge.

@kagan Juo, dat lea juste nu galgá dahkat! Guođđe iežat gáfe dološii, luoitte ihkkapatruvnna... maid beare heive dutnje. Olmmoš dárbbaša dušše oažžut buot dainna fancy ideaiguin mat čájehuvvojit Pinterestas dahje gos beare olggos iežas oaivvis. Dat lea proseassa dokumenteremis iige das ahte galgá leat visuellalaččat fiinna. Geavat dan nugo wrecking journal, jos dat heive. Dat lea ollásit dan olbmo duohken guhte dan ráhkada.

source_doc:
What is it?
It's my daughter, Esther, sir.
You know Ana's girl.
She wants to go to that wedding more than anything.
So it'd mean the world to her.
Well, of course she can attend the latest.
Is that all?
Yes, sir.
Thank you very much, sir.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Danin dat mearkkašivččii sutnje buot.

Leago dat buot?
Lea, hearrá.
Giitu hui olu, hearrá.

source_doc:
As head of this house, I shall arrange for an immediate investigation.
I'd rather not, if you don't mind, sir.
Doctor Sexton, the fact that my brother and sister and I have opened our home to convalescent patients doesn't relieve us of all responsibility for what occurs in it.
I mean, under the peculiar circumstances.
Oh, come along, Bob.
There's no need to shield anyone.

Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. hypo_0:
Dán viesu oaivvildan, mun ordnen dán vahkkumii fargga dutkama, jos ii leat vahága.
Mun mieldehan in háliit, jos dat ii heive, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu viellja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii geavat min buot ovddasvástádusas das mii dáppe dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun jurddašan, dán erenoamáš dili vuolde.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii dárbbaš suodjalit geasage.
hypo_1:
Dán viesu oaiviliin mun ordnen ahte dása álggahuvvo fargga dutkan.
Mun mieluin in dahje, jos dat du mielas ii leat heahtes, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu vielja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii geahpeda min buot ovddasvástádusa das mii dás dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun oaivvildan, dán erenoamáš diliin.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii dárbbaš geasage suodjalit.
hypo_2:
Dán viesu oaivvilin mun ordnen ahte dása álggahuvvo dihkkadeapmi dallánaga.
Mun mieluin in daga dan, jos dat dutnje heive, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu vielja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii merke ahte mii leat luovvan buot ovddasvástádusas das mii dán siste dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun oaivvildan, dán erenoamáš dili vuođul.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii dárbbaš geasage suodjalit.
hypo_3:
Dán viesu oaivámužžan mun fuolahan ahte dasa álggahuvvo ovttalágan fargga dutkan.
Mun mieluin báhcčán das eret, jos dii eahpit, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu vielja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii mearkkaš ahte mii leat luovvan buot ovddasvástádusas das mii dáppe dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun oaivvildan, dán erenoamáš dili vuođul.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii leat mihkkege dárbu geasa ge suodjalit.

Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu vielja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii fuolahit min buot ovddasvástádusas das mii dáppe dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun oaivvildan, dán erenoamáš dili oktavuođas.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii leat mihkkege dárbu geasa ge suodjalit.
hypo_5:
Dán viesu oaivámužžan mun fuolahan ahte dat dutkojuvvo dalánaga.
Mun in mieldege dahkat dan, jos dii eai hehtte, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu viellja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii geahpid min buot ovddasvástádusa das mii dáppe dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun mearkkašan, dán erenoamáš dili vuolde.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii leat dárbu geasa ge suodjalit.
hypo_6:
Dán viesu oaivámužžan mun ordnen ahte dat álggahuvvo fargga iskkadeapmi.
Mun in mielas dahkat dan, jos dii eai bávččas, hearrá.
Doavttir Sexton, dat ahte mu viellja, oabbá ja mun leat rahpan min ruovttu buorráneaddji buohccáide, ii váldde eret min buot ovddasvástádusa das mii dán siste dáhpáhuvvá.
Mun mearkkašan, dán erenoamáš dili vuolde.
A, boađe dál, Bob.
Ii leat dárbu geasa ge suodjalit.
hypo_7:

source_doc:
Reading Gloria Steinem's undercover report on being a Playboy Bunny... finished Part I and damn🤔
Despite being well aware of Playboy being uh... a CORPORATION lol... had no idea how much their entire infrastructure truly served to perpetuate classism in an extremely creepy way while outwardly presenting themselves as the "classy" and thus "more ethical" option compared to say, Hustler

I'll post some screenshots later but to give you the gist, Bunnies (who were cocktail waitresses), had to:
- accept lower (by $200-150, equivalent to today's $2.5K) wages than advertised
- hand over 50% of all their tips
- give their last names to "Number 1 key holders" (ergo, celebrities/elites) if asked
- be subjected to a pelvic exam and blood test but weren't allowed to pick the physician
- be spied on by a private eye
- go out if their way to hide their husbands/bfs

The central point that unites all of this:
Bunnies were STRICTLY NOT ALLOWED TO (penalized by termination) date any other employees or customers... BUT accompanying and dating the "Number 1 key holders" was permitted, if not encouraged. See where this is going?

New recruits were given a "Bunny Bible" to study all those bizarre rules and instructions from.
To mention a few more:
- Bunnies' work performance was assessed by a "merit system" (YES, that exact wording!) instead of being written up. Unsurprisingly, merit was not only judged by a good work ethic but also very specific requirements on looks and attitude.
- there was an incentive system in place that rewarded Bunnies who sold the most liquor and merchandise

source_doc:
Ryan, if you ever feel like you're losing hope and you need a little Christmas miracle, just dial S for Santa.
Ryan, grandma needs you.
Okay, Alana.
I'll be back.
Santa.
It's one thing to fill his head with nonsense about the North Pole, but dial S for Santa.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is automatically transcribed from spoken language and can contain errors. Maintain the flow and colloquial style of the speaker in the translation. Do not include non-linguistic sounds (e.g. laughter, groans, hesitation sounds, etc.), but do include interjections. If a word is interrupted, either guess the full word if possible or otherwise omit it. Keep foreign words as they are when translating. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Output the text such that each sentence is on a separate line. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Ryan, jos don goassege dovddat ahte don leat jávkadeamen doaivvu ja dus lea dárbu unni juovlamirákalii, de dušše ringe S Santii.
Ryan, áhkku dárbbaša du.
Buorre, Alana.
Mun boađán ruovttoluotta.
Santa.
Lea okta ášši deavdit su oaivvi jápmisvuođain Davvi-navddis, muhto ringe S Santii.

Buorre, Alana.
Mun boađán ruovttoluotta.
Santa.
Lea okta ášši deavdit su oaivvi hápmástagaiguin Davvinavddi birra, muhto "vállje S Santii" lea juoidá eará.

Mun boađán ruovttoluotta.
Santa.

source_doc:
Tonight in #MMRPG! My back finally shuts up enough that I can GM again as the heroes continue their search for the missing girl Cammie. Vega the Digital Dragon surprises a trio of mischievous scamps who snuck into her arcade in the middle of the night but one of them recognizes her as a town superhero and they end up having a heartwarming moment geeking out over video games. Unfortunately, the moment is interrupted by somebody or something landing on the roof of the arcade... 1/?

Vega goes to investigate and finds Dr. Vesper, in his monstrous bat form that the press has dubbed the "Gargoyle Man," along with a harpy-like creature that Vesper describes as his "little protegee." Vesper says that they have come to introduce themselves and invite Vega to join their society of man-creature misfits that is led by "The Madame," Dr. Diana Laughton of the Laughton Institute. 2/?

Vega buys some time to get the mischievous scamps out of the arcade and call the rest of the Mythics for backup, then plays along at being interested in joining this "new family." Vesper and Harpy (who does not speak once during the interaction) lead Vega to a ship in the harbor, where the Laughton Institute appears to be packing up their things... presumably because they know they're drawing attention from Agent Carter and A.R.G.E.N.T. among others. 3/?
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Ođđajagi ija #MMRPG:s! Mu cohkki loahpalaččat orru nu ahte sáhtán fas leat GM go herojat joatket ohcan gaskavahkkui jávkan nieidda Cammie geažil. Vega, Digitála Dráhkka, boahtá várra golmma geahččaleaddji váriha, geat suoládit sis geasse eahkedis su arcadea, muhto okta sis dovdá su gávpot-superheroan ja de sis šaddá hui liekkas geekkan video-spealuid birra. Vahágis dat bottu gaskkalduvvo go muhton olbmo dahje juoidá geahččá ruovtti arcadea ala... 1/?

Vega manná dan iskat ja gávdná Dr. Vespera, su monstruosa beavdegirji-hámis, maid mediijat leat nammadan "Gargoyle Man," ja dás maiddái lea harpyja-lágan olmmái, gean Vesper govvida iežas "unna suodjalusin." Vesper dadjá ahte sii leat boahtán iežaset ovddastit ja bovdet Vega mielde searvat sin searvvušii, mii lea olmmoš-heakkalaš misfit-id searvu ja man jođiha "The Madame," Dr. Diana Laughton Laughton Instituhtas. 2/?

Vega ostá iežas veahkkin veahá áiggi vai oažžu dat geahččaleaddji várihat olggos arcadeas ja sáhttá ringet eará Mythics-ide veahkkin, ja de dat niehku mielde nu go livčče beroštan searvat dán "ođđa bearrašii." Vesper ja Harpy (gii ii cealkke oktage sáni olles deaivvadeamis) voddet Vega satnui luotta, gos orru ahte Laughton Instituhta lea bákkeheame iežaset áššiid... vissis danne go sii dihtet ahte Agent Carter ja A.R.G.E.N.T. earáid gaskkas leat álggahan fuomášit sin. 3/?

[

  "Get a piece of the $200B pie with marketplace integrations for eBay and Amazon. Sell seamlessly across the most popular marketplaces right from your Mambalo dashboard.",

source_doc:
My brother in He-Who-Shall-Not-Be-Named, what you are describing is a consequence of _misogyny_, not "misandry."

I desperately need men to realize that just because it's directed at them and an actual serious problem doesn't mean it is "misandry."
Just saw someone describe being discriminated against _for being considered effeminate_ as "misandry" and I just.

It's not that you aren't experiencing something real. You very well may be. But it isn't "misandry." Because "misandry" doesn't exist.
Yes, this applies to trans men as well. "Transmisandry" _also_ isn't a thing and just contributes to anti-feminist narratives. It treats itself as a mirror of transmisogyny, but transmisogyny is _intersective_: we experience transphobia _and_ we experience misogyny _and_ we experience this unholy thing that happens when the two interact. It's not a subset, even an exclusive subset, it's an intersection.
But the following all are absolutely problems that can be talked about!
* Transphobia/Transmisia
* Antitransmasculity
* Transemasculation
* Misogyny
It sucks to be discriminated against, but the language that we use is so, so important here.
instruction: You are a professional Northern Sámi translator, tasked with providing translations suitable for use in Northern Sámi (sme_Latn). Your goal is to accurately convey the meaning and nuances of the original text while adhering to Northern Sámi grammar and vocabulary and ensuring that the translation is natural. The original text is user-generated content from a social media platform. Do not reproduce spelling mistakes. Reproduce marks of expressiveness that communicate meaningful intent (e.g. enthusiasm through capitalisation or elongation) in a way that is natural in Northern Sámi. Copy URLs and user handles directly rather than translating them. However, translate hashtags as appropriate for the translation to be natural for social media text. Follow the punctuation of the source text as best as possible. Additional punctuation should be added only if not doing so would seriously alter the comprehension of the text. Translate text in an informal style, like close friends talking, even if it changes the original tone. Produce only the Northern Sámi translation, without any additional explanations or commentary. Maintain the HTML formatting of the original source text. Please translate the following text into Northern Sámi (sme_Latn):
hypo_0:
Mu viellja Das-Gii-Ii-Galgga-Leahttut-Namai, dat maid don válddát govvidit lea _nissonvašuheapmi_, ii “almmáivašuheapmi”.

Mun dárbbašan ihan dušše ahte albmát fuomášivčče ahte dat go juoga čuohcá sidjiide ja lea duohta, duođalaš váttisvuohta, ii mearkkaš ahte dat lea “almmáivašuheapmi”.
Oainnán aiddo muhtumaš olbmo gohčodit dan ahte son diskriminerejuvvui _dan geažil ahte su leat oaidnán nissonlaš_ “almmáivašuheamen” ja mun juste.

Ii leat nu ahte don it leat vásihan juoidá duohta. Dat sáhttá hui bures leat nu. Muhto dat ii leat “almmáivašuheapmi”. Danne go “almmáivašuheapmi” ii leat olusge.
Juo, dát gusto transalbmáide maid. “Transalmmáivašuheapmi” _maiddái_ ii leat mihkkege ja dušše nanne antifeministtalaš narratiivvaid. Dat gieđahallá iežas dego transmisogyniija speadjalin, muhto transmisogyniija lea _čatnasančoahkkáigeassu_: mii vásihit transfobiija _ja_ mii vásihit nissonvašuheami _ja_ mii vásihit dán eahpelunddolaš ášši mii šaddá go dát guokte deaivvadit. Dat ii leat oassi, ii juobe erenoamáš oassi, dat lea čoahkkáigeassu.
Muhto čuovvovaččat buohkat leat duođaid váttisvuođat maid sáhttá hupmat birra!
* Transfobiija/Transmisia
* Antitransmaskulinitehta
* Transemaskuleren
* Nissonvašuheapmi
Lea ihan veadjemeahttun go diskriminerejuvvo, muhto giella maid geavahit dáppe lea nu, nu dehálaš.
hypo_1:
Mu viellja Sus-Gii-Ii-Galgga-Namai-Hálddašit, dat maid don leat govvemin lea _nissonvašuheami_ boađus, ii “almmáiavašuheapmi”.

Mun dárbbašan duođaid ahte albmát ipmirdivčče ahte dat go juoga čuohcá sidjiide ja lea duohta duođalaš váttisvuohta, ii mearkkaš ahte dat lea “almmáiavašuheapmi”.
Oainnán áiddo muhtun olbmo gohčodit dan ahte son lea diskriminerejuvvon _dan geažil ahte su leat doallán nissonlágan_ “almmáiavašuheamen” ja mun juste.

Ii leat nu ahte don it leat vásiheamen juoidá duohta. Dat sáhttá hui bures leat nu. Muhto dat ii leat “almmáiavašuheapmi”. Danne go “almmáiavašuheapmi” ii leat mihkkege.
Juo, dát gusto transalbmáide maid. “Transalmmáiavašuheapmi” _maiddái_ ii leat mihkkege ja dušše veahkeha antifeministtalaš narratiivaid. Dat gieđahallá iežas dego transmisogyniija speadjalin, muhto transmisogyniija lea _ruossastan_: mii vásihit transfobiija _ja_ mii vásihit nissonvašuheami _ja_ mii vásihit dán eahpelunddolaš ášši mii šaddá go dát guokte deaivvadit. Dat ii leat vuolledoaimma, ii juobe erenoamáš vuolledoaimma, dat lea ruossastan.
Muhto čuovvovaččat leat buohkat duođaid váttisvuođat maid sáhttá hupmat birra!
* Transfobiija/Transmisia
* Antitransmaskuliniteta
* Transemaskuleren
* Nissonvašuheapmi
Lea suohttasaheapme go diskriminerejuvvo, muhto giella maid mii geavahit dáppe lea hui, hui dehálaš.
hypo_2:
Mu viellja Sus-gii-ii-galgga-namai-mainna, dat maid don leat govvemin lea _nissonvašuheami_ bohtos, ii “almmáiavašuheapmi”.

Mun duođaid dárbbašan ahte albmát ipmirdit ahte dat go juoga čuohcá sidjiide ja lea álggosge duođalaš váttisvuohta, ii dasto mearkkaš ahte dat lea “almmáiavašuheapmi”.
Oainnán áidna easka muhtun olbmo gohčodit dan ahte son lea diskriminerejuvvon _dan geažil go su leat doallan nissonlágan_ “almmáiavašuheamen” ja mun juste.

Ii leat nu ahte don it leat vásiheamen juoidá duohta. Dat sáhttá hui bures leat nu. Muhto dat ii leat “almmáiavašuheapmi”. Danne go “almmáiavašuheapmi” ii leat mihkkege.
Juo, dát gusto transalbmáide maid. “Transalmmáiavašuheapmi” _maiddái_ ii leat mihkkege ja dušše addá fámu antifeministtalaš narratiivvaide. Dat bidjá iežas dego transmisogyniija speadjalin, muhto transmisogyniija lea _ruossasteaddji_: mii vásihit transfobiija _ja_ mii vásihit nissonvašuheami _ja_ mii vásihit dán eahpelunddolaš ášši mii šaddá go dat guokte deaivvadit. Dat ii leat vuolledoaimma, ii juobe dakkár erenoamáš vuolledoaimma, dat lea ruossastan.
Muhto čuovvovaččat leat buohkat duođaid váttisvuođat maid sáhttá hupmat birra!
